title: Practice problem - Transform the Expression

content.problemStatement:
Reverse polish notations are easier to parse for computers and you don't need parentheses to denote precedence of operations.


Transform the algebraic expression with brackets into RPN form. 



You can assume that for the test cases below only single letters will be used, brackets [] will not be used and each expression has only one RPN form (no expressions like a*b*c)



Input Format

The first line contains t, the number of test cases (less then 100).
Followed by t lines, containing an expression to be translated to RPN form, where the length of the expression is less then 400.


Output Format
The expressions in RPN form, one per line.
constraints: Only single lower‑case or upper‑case letters are used as operands
Parentheses are used to make the order of evaluation unambiguous
Each expression has a unique postfix representation
content.inputFormat: First line contains an integer T, the number of test cases (1 ≤ T ≤ 100). Each of the next T lines contains a single fully parenthesised algebraic expression consisting of single‑letter operands, the binary operators +, -, *, /, ^ and parentheses '(' and ')'. The length of each expression is less than 400 characters.
content.outputFormat: For each test case output the corresponding expression in Reverse Polish Notation (postfix form) on its own line.

solution.approach: The classic way to convert an infix expression with explicit parentheses to postfix is to use a stack for operators. Scan the expression from left to right. • If the current character is an operand, append it to the output. • If it is '(', push it onto the stack. • If it is ')', pop operators from the stack and append them until '(' is encountered; discard the '(' . • If it is an operator, pop and output any operators on the stack that have higher or equal precedence (because the current operator will be applied later) and then push the current operator. After the scan, pop any remaining operators. Because the input is fully parenthesised, the algorithm never faces ambiguity and produces the unique RPN form. The precedence order used is: ^ (highest, right‑associative but treated as left‑associative here because parentheses remove ambiguity), then * and /, then + and -. The algorithm runs in O(|S|) time per test case and uses O(|S|) auxiliary space for the stack and output string.
solution.timeComplexity: O(L) per test case, where L is the length of the expression; O(total length) for the whole input.
solution.spaceComplexity: O(L) auxiliary space for the operator stack and the resulting postfix string.

tcGeneratorPython:
def generate_case(i, RINT, POWER):
    # i is the test case index (0‑based). Print the complete input for that case.
    if i==0:
        t=1
        print(t)
        print('(a)')
    elif i==1:
        t=1
        print(t)
        print('(a+b)')
    elif i==2:
        t=1
        print(t)
        print('(a^b)')
    elif i==3:
        t=1
        print(t)
        print('((a))')
    elif i==4:
        t=1
        print(t)
        print('(a+b-c*d/e^f+g-h*i+j)')
    elif i==5:
        t=1
        print(t)
        print('(((a+b)*(c+d))^((e-f)/(g+h)))')
    elif i==6:
        t=1
        print(t)
        print('(a+(b+(c+(d+e))))')
    elif i==7:
        t=1
        print(t)
        print('(((a)))')
    elif i==8:
        t=1
        print(t)
        print('(((a+b)+c)+d)')
    elif i==9:
        t=1
        print(t)
        print('(((a*b)*c)*d)')
    elif i==10:
        t=1
        print(t)
        print('(a+(b*(c+(d/e))))')
    elif i==11:
        t=1
        print(t)
        print('(((a^b)-c)+d)')
    elif i==12:
        t=1
        print(t)
        print('(a+(b*(c-(d/(e+f)^g))))')
    elif i==13:
        t=1
        print(t)
        print('((a+b)*(c-(d/e)))')
    elif i==14:
        t=1
        print(t)
        print('((a+(b^c))-((d+e)*f))')
    elif i==15:
        t=1
        print(t)
        print('(((a+b)-c)^(d+e*f))')
    elif i==16:
        t=3
        print(t)
        print('(a+b)')
        print('(a-(b*c))')
        print('((a+b)*(c+d))')
    elif i==17:
        t=4
        print(t)
        print('(a+b*c)')
        print('((a+b)/c)')
        print('(a^(b+c))')
        print('((a+b)-(c+d))')
    elif i==18:
        t=5
        print(t)
        print('(a)')
        print('(b)')
        print('(c)')
        print('(d)')
        print('(e)')
    elif i==19:
        t=2
        print(t)
        print('(((a+b)*(c+d))*(e+f))')
        print('(((a+b)+(c+d))+(e+f))')
    else:
        # default empty case
        print(0)
